summary.intent: Enhance the checkpoint/branch log UI with auto-scroll on checkpoint selection, diff2html file changes view, and a proper server-side API for fetching checkpoint-specific log events.
summary.outcome: Added FilesChangedPanel (collapsible diff2html view) to both SessionDetail and BranchLog, created a new server route and API function for checkpoint-scoped log events replacing timestamp-based filtering, then removed the auto-scroll behavior the user decided they didn't want.

repo: LogEvent rows have a sessionLinkId foreign key → SessionLink → CheckpointMetadata.checkpointId, making it possible to query log events by checkpoint via a join chain rather than timestamp filtering.
BranchLog.tsx had a timestamp-based filterEventsForEntry heuristic that was unreliable; the proper join path through CheckpointMetadata is now used instead.
FilesChangedPanel was defined in SessionDetail.tsx and exported so BranchLog.tsx could reuse it — shared UI components for checkpoint views live in SessionDetail.
Session.cwd is string | null, used as localPath for diff rendering.
The 'live' session entry in BranchLog has no checkpoint, so it must continue using fetchLogEvents rather than the new checkpoint-scoped route.

code.finding: FilesChangedPanel is exported from here and reused in BranchLog; uses key={selectedId} to auto-reset collapsed state on checkpoint switch.
loadRightPanel now calls fetchCheckpointLogEvents(entry.sessionId, entry.checkpointId); loadLivePanel still uses fetchLogEvents.
New route GET /api/v2/sessions/:sessionId/checkpoints/:checkpointId/log-events filters LogEvent via the SessionLink→CheckpointMetadata join.

workflow: Auto-scroll was added and then immediately removed in the same session after the user reconsidered — worth confirming scroll behavior preferences before implementing.
Exporting a component from one screen file to reuse in another is the current pattern rather than extracting to a shared components directory.

friction: Multiple read passes of the same files were needed to locate the correct insertion points for edits, suggesting these files are large and complex.
FilesChangedPanel had to be exported from SessionDetail after initially being defined as a local component, requiring a second edit pass.
The scroll feature was implemented fully and then removed, wasting implementation effort — the user's intent wasn't fully clarified upfront.
open_items: FilesChangedPanel is co-located in SessionDetail.tsx rather than a shared components file — could be moved if more screens need it.
The diff fetch in FilesChangedPanel is lazy (on expand); no caching is in place if the user collapses and reopens the same checkpoint.
Live session panel in BranchLog still uses the timestamp-based fetchLogEvents path — no checkpoint-scoped equivalent exists for in-progress sessions.
